title: Key concepts: The Srivijaya Empire
description: Check your knowledge on the history of the Srivijaya Empire!

assessment_items.question_content:
Historians have learned a lot about the Srivijaya Empire through

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Surviving written records from the Srivijaya Empire only exist from a small window of time in the seventh century.

choices.content: detailed accounts from the Srivijaya empire itself from the seventh through the twelfth centuries
accounts of trade that merchants from China and India logged
oral tradition passed down from one Srivijaya generation to the next in Old Malay

assessment_items.question_content:
According to the article, the use of a common language, Old Malay, helped people in the Srivijaya Empire

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The article doesn’t mention anything about monument building projects.

choices.content: attack other states trying to grab power

assessment_items.question_content:
One way Srivijaya rulers expanded their power was by

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Srivijaya Empire was a vassal state to the Song dynasty, not the other way around.

choices.content: requiring that everyone in the empire become fluent in Old Malay
visiting the court of the Song dynasty and demanding they become a vassal state to the empire

assessment_items.question_content:
Historians can point to a possible cultural syncretism, or blending, between India and the Srivijaya empire because of

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the development of Vajrayana Hinduism in both places around the same time
the development of Vajrayana Buddhism in both places around the same time
the Chola Empire’s attack on the Srivijaya Empire and domination of sea lanes

assessment_items.question_content:
Explorers like Zheng He’s use of maritime trade routes near the Srivijaya Empire long after the Srivijayans lost power indicates


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Zheng He was one of _many_ explorers and traders who continued to use these sea lanes after the Srivijaya Empire declined.

choices.content: the sea lanes the Srivijayans built up continued to be useful after the Srivijaya empire’s decline
the Chinese found alternate routes to Arabia and beyond after the Srivijaya sea lanes collapsed
Zheng He was one of the few people to successfully navigate the waters near the former Srivijaya empire after it declined
